content: Common sourdough starter mistakes that prevent rising: Overfeeding depletes yeast population before it can establish - young starters (under 2 weeks) need patience, not more food. Using whole grain flour exclusively can make starter too acidic; blend with bread flour. Container too narrow prevents observing rise - use wide-mouth jar. Starter isn't hungry enough at feeding time - if it peaks and falls before next feeding, increase feeding frequency. Environmental factors: altitude affects fermentation (slower above 3000ft), seasonal humidity changes hydration needs. Contamination from dirty utensils introduces competing microbes. Don't discard all starter each feeding - keep some mature culture for microbial diversity. If starter develops pink/orange streaks or smells putrid, discard and start over - harmful bacteria present.
source: Perfect Loaf Sourdough Troubleshooting & Bread Lab Research
